First, I'll read through Brad's response again to understand his main points. Brad starts by acknowledging that it's a difficult question but offers his views. He mentions that his sense of humor developed over time, going from not being funny to being able to make people laugh. He also talks about the importance of language fluency, seeing irony, laughing frequently, appreciating others' jokes, and finding humor in everyday situations. He also says that building confidence and developing one's own style is important. The key part here is when Brad says that his own sense of humor developed over time. This suggests that he's trying to convey that humor isn't something innate but can be developed through practice and experience. He gives examples of how he improved, like watching comedies, practicing by noticing situations, and so on. This supports the idea that sense of humor can be developed.
